label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Thomas could have gone to the football field. First, I need to look at all the times he was seen doing other things and see when he was free. So, Thomas woke up at 8am. Then David saw him at the electronic store fixing his computer from 8am to 10am. That means he was busy from 8 to 10. Next, Jennifer saw him buying lunch from 10am to 12pm. So he was at the deli during that time. Then Lisa saw him walking towards the Statue of Liberty from 12pm to 2pm. So that's another activity. After that, Sarah saw him buying a phone at the electronics store from 2pm to 5pm. Then Kimberly saw him buying a bike from 6pm to 9pm. The football field is closed after 9pm, so he had to go before that. Now, let's list out his occupied times: - 8am-10am: fixing computer - 10am-12pm: buying lunch - 12pm-2pm: going to Statue of Liberty - 2pm-5pm: buying phone - 6pm-9pm: buying bike So the times he's free would be between the end of one activity and the start of the next. Let's check the gaps. From when he wakes up at 8am until 8am, there's no activity before that. But he woke up at 8am, so maybe he could have done something right away? But David saw him at 8am already. So from 8am onwards, he's occupied. After 10am (end of computer fixing) until 10am (start of lunch), but lunch starts at 10am, so there's no gap there. Then after lunch ends at 12pm, next is Statue of Liberty from 12pm, so again no gap. After that ends at 2pm, he goes to buy a phone from 2pm to 5pm. Still no gap. After 5pm, the next activity is at 6pm (bike shop). So between 5pm and 6pm, he's free. Then after 9pm, the field is closed. So the only free time is between 5pm and 6pm. Let me check the options again. Option D is 5pm to 6pm. So that's the answer. Let me make sure I didn't miss any other gaps. From waking up at 8am, he's busy until 10am. Then busy until 12pm, then until 2pm, then until 5pm. Then free until 6pm.
